title: fine-tuned llm as program writers
summary: This blog post explores the use of fine-tuned large language models (LLMs) as universal 'program-writers' for program synthesis. It argues that LLMs can overcome the need for domain-specific synthesizers by learning programmatic patterns, understanding human language conventions, and reliably generating syntactically complex programs from textual specifications. The post details a practical experiment using the byT5-base model, which is fine-tuned on in-domain (specification, program) pairs for a rectangle description problem. The experiments demonstrate that a lightweight, fine-tuned LLM can achieve high performance in generating correct programs, outperforming previous baselines, by simply manipulating strings and integrating the model into a synthesis loop.
author: evan (evanthebouncy)
